title: U.S. Leads Brazil in Corn Exports to China
section_path: U.S. Leads Brazil in Corn Exports to China
section_path_text: U.S. Leads Brazil in Corn Exports to China
summary:
Main
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-03-20 113701.png | assets/2026-03-20_us-leads-brazil-in-corn-exports-to-china_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_62ff6e6b8de8.png
Key Takeaways
● China's corn flows are shifting in early 2026. Brazil dominated China's corn imports in 2025, but early Q1 2026 data indicate the U.S. ● Dry bulk sentiment improved week over week.

children.title: U.S. Overtakes Brazil in Corn Exports to China Ahead of End-Q1 2026
section_path: U.S. Overtakes Brazil in Corn Exports to China Ahead of End-Q1 2026
children.section_path_text: U.S. Overtakes Brazil in Corn Exports to China Ahead of End-Q1 2026
children.summary:
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-03-20 113725.png | assets/2026-03-20_us-leads-brazil-in-corn-exports-to-china_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_37f5cacfdc7f.png
Chinese corn imports declined to approximately 3.8M mt in 2025, down from 8.4M mt in 2024. Of this, around 2.5M mt originated from Brazil (Panamax: ~2.0M mt vs.

children.title: Freight Market Overview
children.summary:
The Baltic Dry Index (BDI) recorded an uptick in sentiment before the end of the week, with the index value moving above the 2,000-point mark, driven by weekly gains in the Capesize and Panamax segments. Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-03-20 113757.png | assets/2026-03-20_us-leads-brazil-in-corn-exports-to-china_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_742805d64d86.png
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-03-20 113816.png | assets/2026-03-20_us-leads-brazil-in-corn-exports-to-china_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_a0f702ef67e3.png
The rate for the Tubarao to Qingdao route held the firmer sentiment of the previous week, with rates still around $30/mt (+23% YoY).
